title: Friendly Health Advice
description: Give a friend advice about healthy habits using suggestion grammar.
canDo: Can give and respond to advice about daily habits using ~たほうがいい and ~ないほうがいい.
participants: Friend who has been feeling tired lately
sampleDialogue: 最近疲れているなら、夜遅くまでスマホを見ないほうがいいですよ。
practicePrompts: Your friend says they have been feeling tired. Suggest they should sleep earlier using ~たほうがいい.
Your friend asks about diet. Advise them not to skip breakfast using ~ないほうがいい.
Respond to your friend's thanks by adding one more suggestion for staying healthy.

title: Responding to Unsolicited Advice
titleJa: 求めていないアドバイスへの返し方
description: Politely push back on or negotiate advice you did not ask for, while keeping the conversation friendly.
canDo: Can politely question or reframe advice received using ~たほうがいい and ~ないほうがいい in a negotiated exchange.
sampleDialogue: アドバイスはありがたいんですが、毎日運動するのはちょっと難しいですね。週に三回ならできるかもしれません。
practicePrompts: Your acquaintance tells you that you should stop eating late at night. Acknowledge the advice but explain why it is difficult for you right now.
Suggest a modified version of their advice that is more realistic for your lifestyle, using ~たほうがいい.
Wrap up the exchange by thanking them and sharing one piece of counter-advice about not overdoing exercise.

title: Passing Along the News
description: Report what you heard or read to a classmate using indirect speech.
canDo: Can report and summarize information heard from others using indirect speech.
sampleDialogue: 先生が来週の授業はオンラインだと言っていました。
practicePrompts: Tell your classmate what the teacher said about next week's class schedule.
Report something you read online about a campus event using ~そうです.
Your classmate shares news. Summarize it back and ask a follow-up question.

title: Playing Telephone
description: Relay a piece of information through a chain of people, noticing how details shift with each retelling.
canDo: Can relay second-hand information accurately and flag uncertainty about what was originally said.
participants: Chain of two or three classmates
sampleDialogue: 田中さんから聞いたんですが、山田さんが来週パーティーを開くと言っていたそうです。
practicePrompts: You heard from a friend that the school festival has been moved to a different date. Tell the next person in the chain, making clear it is second-hand information.
The next person has already heard a slightly different version. Compare the two accounts and decide which detail seems correct.
Discuss how the information changed along the chain and reflect on why indirect speech can become distorted.

title: Planning for the Future
description: Explain your goals and what you are doing to achieve them.
canDo: Can explain goals and the steps being taken to reach them using ~ために and ~ように.
sampleDialogue: 日本で働けるように、今ビジネス日本語を勉強しています。
practicePrompts: Tell your senpai about a career goal and what you are doing to prepare using ~ために.
Explain a habit you started so that something would become possible using ~ように.
Ask your senpai what they did in order to achieve their own goal.

title: Explaining a Setback
description: Describe an obstacle that got in the way of a goal and explain how you adjusted your approach.
canDo: Can explain why a plan did not work out and describe a revised approach using ~ように.
sampleDialogue: 試験に落ちてしまったので、今度は毎日少しずつ勉強できるように、スケジュールを変えました。
practicePrompts: Tell your friend about a goal you set and the unexpected difficulty that got in the way.
Explain what you changed in your approach so the problem would not happen again, using ~ように.
Ask your friend about a time they had to revise their plans and how it worked out.

title: Choosing a Part-Time Job
description: Discuss and compare part-time job options with a friend.
canDo: Can discuss priorities and make comparisons using ~より and ~ほど.
participants: Friend looking for a part-time job
sampleDialogue: カフェのバイトはコンビニより楽しそうだけど、給料はコンビニほど高くないよ。
practicePrompts: Compare two part-time jobs your friend is considering using ~より.
Explain that one option is not as good as another in a specific way using ~ほど…ない.
State which job you would choose and explain your priorities.

title: Evaluating Two Job Offers
description: Compare two job offers using multiple criteria beyond salary, such as location, work culture, and growth opportunities.
canDo: Can compare two options across multiple dimensions and weigh trade-offs using ~より and ~ほど.
sampleDialogue: 給料はA社のほうが高いけど、B社のほうが残業が少なくて、職場の雰囲気も良さそうだよ。
practicePrompts: You have two job offers. Compare them on at least three criteria — such as commute time, atmosphere, and career growth — using ~より and ~ほど.
Your friend points out a factor you have not considered. Respond by saying it is not as important to you as another factor.
Make your final recommendation to yourself out loud, explaining which offer is better overall and why.

title: Talking About Social Problems
description: Discuss a social issue you care about with a discussion partner.
canDo: Can describe and discuss social issues using ~について and ~に対して.
sampleDialogue: 最近の少子化問題について、どう思いますか。
practicePrompts: Bring up a social issue you find important using ~について.
Share your opinion or concern regarding the issue using ~に対して.
Ask your partner what they think society should do about the problem.

title: Proposing a Solution
description: Suggest a practical fix for a social problem and defend it when your partner raises objections.
canDo: Can propose a concrete solution to a social issue and respond to counterarguments using ~について and ~に対して.
sampleDialogue: 食品ロスの問題に対して、スーパーが値引きシールを早めに貼るようにすればいいと思います。
practicePrompts: Introduce the social problem you want to address using ~について, giving one or two key facts.
Propose a specific, realistic solution and explain why you think it would work, using ~に対して.
Your partner raises a practical objection to your idea. Acknowledge the point and refine your proposal.

title: How I Have Changed
description: Reflect on personal growth and changes since starting to study Japanese.
canDo: Can reflect on personal changes and growth using ~ようになった and ~てきた.
sampleDialogue: 日本語を始めてから、日本のドラマが少しずつわかるようになってきました。
practicePrompts: Describe something you can do now that you could not do before using ~ようになった.
Talk about a gradual change you have noticed in yourself using ~てきた.
Ask your partner how they have changed since they started learning a new skill.

title: What I Want to Become
description: Share your aspirations for the future and describe the concrete steps you are actively taking to get there.
canDo: Can describe personal aspirations and ongoing efforts toward them using ~ようになりたい and ~てきた.
sampleDialogue: 将来は自分で事業を始められるようになりたいので、今は少しずつ経営の勉強をしてきています。
practicePrompts: Describe the kind of person you want to become in five years, using ~ようになりたい.
Explain what you have already started doing to move in that direction, using ~てきた.
Ask your partner about their own aspirations and what they have been doing to work toward them.
